Off Calendar Apr-24-2025 Text Ruling

DEFENDANT DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of The Issues.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-19-2024 Continued To May-19-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of The Issues
Off Calendar Sep-19-2024 Continued To Apr-24-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint; Memorandum In Support; And Declaration Of Nicholas W. Hane, Esq.
Off Calendar Oct-04-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JULIA MALDONADO's Motion for Leave to File a First Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion is ordered off calendar for failure to comply with Local Rule 2.7(B) (courtesy copies).

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/CK)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-11-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JULIA MALDONADO's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT.

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file first amended complaint is denied for failure to comply with CRC 3.1324(b).

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF EXPERT WITNESS
Off Calendar Sep-03-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Nick Hane, of California Employment Counsel, APC, Tel. (714) 462-8376, for plaintiffs, appears via Zoom;
No other appearances in the matter.

RULING: The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JULIA MALDONADO MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITION OF EXPERT WITNESS is DENIED without prejudice. The topics, as phrased, are overbroad. Based on the prior discovery orders, Defendant was required to answer interrogatories about potential "me too" evidence. The requests should be tailored to the answers to those responses. Sanctions DENIED. Both parties have reasonable interpretations of the scope of discovery and so acted with substantial justification.

PLAINTIFF JULIA MALDONADO MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROG, SET ONE TO DEFT DELTA DENTAL (TENTATIVE RULING PART 1 SEE PART 2)
Off Calendar Jul-09-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, July 10, 2024, line 3,

PLAINTIFF JULIA MALDONADO MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROG, SET ONE TO DEFT DELTA DENTAL (TENTATIVE RULING PART 2of 2)

For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPARTMENT 302 DAILY AT 9:00 A.M.), or dial the corresponding number and use the meeting ID, and password for Discovery Department 302.

Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem.

The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (end of tentativer ruling part 2 of 2)
